questions.question_text: Dr. Al-Mansouri has been 'pioneering' an innovative approach to treating phobias. What does 'pioneering' mean?

options.text: Following and copying methods that other doctors already use
Developing and leading new methods that others will later adopt
Studying and documenting methods without testing their effectiveness
Criticising and finding faults in existing therapeutic approaches

questions.explanation_ar: pioneering تعني يُرسّخ ويكون رائداً، أي تطوير طرق جديدة يفتح بها الطريق أمام الآخرين ليتبعوا.

questions.question_text: VRET represents a 'significant' breakthrough in how therapists help patients. What does 'significant' mean?

options.text: Very minor and barely noticeable in its impact
Important, meaningful, and worth paying attention to
Controversial and rejected by most professional experts
Theoretical and not yet tested in real clinical settings

questions.explanation_ar: significant تعني مهم وذو دلالة كبيرة، أي أن الشيء يستحق الانتباه لأثره الملموس الواضح.

questions.question_text: VRET helps patients gradually 'confront' their fears in a safe virtual space. What does 'confront' mean?

options.text: To avoid and run away from a frightening situation
To face and deal with something difficult directly
To study and analyse a problem from a safe distance
To imagine and visualise a situation without actually facing it

questions.explanation_ar: confront تعني يواجه مباشرةً، أي الجرأة على مواجهة شيء صعب أو مخيف بدلاً من التهرب منه.

questions.question_text: The virtual environment is 'appealing' to Saudi patients because of its privacy. What does 'appealing' mean?

options.text: Confusing and difficult to understand or navigate
Unattractive and generally avoided by most patients
Attractive and interesting in a way that draws people toward it
Expensive and affordable only by wealthy patients

questions.explanation_ar: appealing تعني جذاب وجاذب، أي أن الشيء يمتلك خصائص تجعل الناس مُنجذبين إليه ومُهتمّين به.

questions.question_text: Many avoid seeking help due to the 'stigma' associated with mental health issues. What does 'stigma' mean?

options.text: A medical certificate that allows access to mental health services
A negative social label that causes shame and discourages people
A positive social recognition of someone's personal achievement
A professional qualification held by mental health doctors

questions.explanation_ar: stigma تعني وصمة عار اجتماعية، أي تصنيف سلبي مرتبط بحالة معينة يجعل الناس يشعرون بالخجل ويتجنبون طلب المساعدة.

questions.question_text: A 'secure' therapy setting is one where patients feel safe, protected, and free from judgment.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. secure تعني آمن ومطمئن، أي أن البيئة تُوفّر الحماية والشعور بالأمان الذي يُمكّن المريض من التعامل مع قضاياه بحرية.

questions.question_text: When patients 'work through' their anxieties, they avoid the issue entirely until it disappears on its own.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. work through تعني يعالج بجهد ومثابرة، أي مواجهة الصعوبات والتعامل معها تدريجياً — وهو عكس الهروب أو التجنّب.

questions.question_text: The wide _____ of VRET extend beyond treating phobias to include post-traumatic stress disorder and anxiety.
questions.explanation_ar: implications تعني تداعيات ونتائج محتملة، أي الآثار والتأثيرات الأوسع التي تترتب على اكتشاف أو تطبيق معين.

questions.question_text: Convert: Dr. Al-Mansouri asked, 'Do patients feel safer in the virtual environment?'

options.text: Dr. Al-Mansouri asked if patients felt safer in the virtual environment.
Dr. Al-Mansouri asked if patients feel safer in the virtual environment.
Dr. Al-Mansouri asked do patients felt safer in the environment.
Dr. Al-Mansouri asked that patients felt safer in the virtual environment.

questions.explanation_ar: في الأسئلة المنقولة: asked + if/whether + subject + verb (بدون صيغة سؤال). feel → felt (تحوّل الزمن). الخيار a صحيح.

questions.question_text: Which sentence correctly uses reported speech where 'that' is optional?

options.text: She said that the therapy was effective, and she said was confident.
She said the therapy was effective.
She said therapy the was effective.
She said that the therapy is effective.

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول يمكن حذف 'that' وتظل الجملة صحيحة: She said (that) the therapy was effective — كلاهما مقبول.

questions.question_text: Convert: The therapist said, 'The patient can practise at home.'

options.text: The therapist said that the patient can practise at home.
The therapist said that the patient could practise at home.
The therapist told the patient can practise at home.
The therapist said that the patient would practise at home.

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول الماضي: can → could. الخيار b صحيح. الخيار c خاطئ (told يحتاج مفعولاً به)، الخيار d يُغيّر المعنى (would = will).

questions.question_text: Convert: The researcher said, 'Today we are seeing remarkable results with VRET.'

options.text: The researcher said that today they were seeing remarkable results.
The researcher said that that day they were seeing remarkable results.
The researcher said that today they are seeing remarkable results.
The researcher told that that day they saw remarkable results.

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول تتغير: today → that day، are seeing → were seeing. الخيار b يطبّق كلا التحوّلين بشكل صحيح.

questions.question_text: The reported speech version of 'He asked: Are the results improving?' is: 'He asked whether the results were improving.'
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. في الأسئلة المنقولة نستخدم whether/if بدلاً من Are، ونُحوّل الزمن: are improving → were improving.

questions.question_text: Match each reported question to its original direct speech form.

left_items: She asked whether the patient had completed all eight sessions.
He asked how long the therapy had been running in Riyadh.
The journalist asked if VR had changed mental health treatment.
The doctor asked what the patient was most afraid of.
right_items: 'Has the patient completed all eight sessions?'
'How long has the therapy been running in Riyadh?'

correct_answer.left: She asked whether the patient had completed all eight sessions.
correct_answer.right: 'Has the patient completed all eight sessions?'

correct_answer.left: He asked how long the therapy had been running in Riyadh.
correct_answer.right: 'How long has the therapy been running in Riyadh?'

correct_answer.left: The journalist asked if VR had changed mental health treatment.
The doctor asked what the patient was most afraid of.

questions.explanation_ar: في الأسئلة المنقولة: نُزيل علامات الاستفهام، نُغيّر الزمن للخلف، ونستخدم if/whether للأسئلة المغلقة وكلمة الاستفهام للأسئلة المفتوحة.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, what percentage of VRET patients experienced substantial improvement in their phobia symptoms?
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن البحث المُجرى في جامعة ستانفورد أثبت أن 83% من المرضى الذين أتمّوا جلسات VRET حقّقوا تحسّناً ملحوظاً في أعراض الرهاب.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, what makes VRET particularly appealing to Saudi patients?

options.text: It is the cheapest form of psychological therapy available
It provides privacy and cultural sensitivity in a secure setting
It requires fewer sessions than traditional therapy methods
It guarantees a complete cure with no need for follow-up

questions.explanation_ar: أوضح النص أن الخصوصية والحساسية الثقافية للبيئة الافتراضية تجعلها جذابة للمرضى السعوديين الذين يتجنبون طلب المساعدة بسبب الوصمة الاجتماعية.

questions.question_text: Passage B describes brain imaging studies showing that VRET decreases activity in the amygdala. What does this suggest about the treatment?

options.text: VRET damages the amygdala permanently, eliminating all fear responses
VRET physically removes the amygdala from the brain
VRET produces measurable neurological changes, reducing fear responses
VRET only affects conscious thinking and has no impact on brain structure

questions.explanation_ar: تكشف دراسات التصوير الدماغي أن العلاج بالتعرض الافتراضي يُحدث تغييرات عصبية حقيقية وقابلة للقياس تُقلّص نشاط اللوزة الدماغية المسؤولة عن استجابات الخوف.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, Dr. Al-Mansouri's VRET work at the National Centre for Mental Health in Riyadh has attracted global attention from medical professionals.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. ذكر النص أن نهجها الرائد في المركز الوطني للصحة النفسية بالرياض حقّق معدلات نجاح رائعة استقطبت انتباه المهنيين الطبيين حول العالم.

questions.question_text: According to Passage B, _____ percent of patients who completed VRET sessions showed substantial improvement in phobia symptoms.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن بحث ستانفورد أثبت أن 83% من المرضى الذين أتمّوا جلسات VRET حقّقوا تحسّناً جوهرياً في أعراضهم.

questions.question_text: Match each key finding or feature of VRET from Passage B to its description.

right_items: reason VRET is especially suited to Saudi patients' needs
Dr. Al-Mansouri's emphasis on the importance of the therapist relationship
neurological evidence that VRET changes brain patterns
VRET success rate compared to 67% in traditional therapy

correct_answer.right: VRET success rate compared to 67% in traditional therapy
neurological evidence that VRET changes brain patterns
reason VRET is especially suited to Saudi patients' needs
Dr. Al-Mansouri's emphasis on the importance of the therapist relationship

questions.explanation_ar: وردت هذه الحقائق في النص: نسبة النجاح 83% مقارنةً بـ 67%، التغيّر العصبي في اللوزة، الخصوصية الثقافية، وتأكيد الدكتورة على أهمية العلاقة الإنسانية.
